archimate:Grouping.name: Containerization Enablers
archimate:Grouping.documentation:
Definition: Containerization Enablers ABB is a Grouping that refers to components and services that facilitate the packaging of applications into standardized units for development, shipment and deployment. This grouping includes two main elements: the Container and the Containers Orchestrator.
Source: EIRA Team Research
Source reference: N/A
Additional information: Containerization Enablers ABB is a crucial component in modern software development and deployment processes. It includes the '[Container]' and '[Containers Orchestrator]' elements. The '[Container]' is a lightweight and portable software package that includes everything needed to run an application, ensuring a consistent and isolated environment for applications to run across different computing environments. The '[Containers Orchestrator]' is a software tool or platform that automates the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications, providing a centralized control plane to efficiently manage and coordinate the deployment and operation of containers across a cluster of machines or nodes.
Example: An example of Containerization Enablers ABB in action is the use of Docker as a container platform to package an application and its dependencies into a single object, and Kubernetes as a container orchestrator to manage the deployment and scaling of the Docker containers across a cluster of servers in a cloud environment. This allows for efficient, reliable, and scalable deployment of applications.
LOST view: TVA-Containerization Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/ContainerizationEnablersGrouping
ABB name: egovera:ContainerizationEnablersGrouping
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/ContainerizationEnablersGrouping
Name: Containerization EnablersGrouping
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication

properties.value: Containerization Enablers ABB is a Grouping that refers to components and services that facilitate the packaging of applications into standardized units for development, shipment and deployment. This grouping includes two main elements: the Container and the Containers Orchestrator.

An example of Containerization Enablers ABB in action is the use of Docker as a container platform to package an application and its dependencies into a single object, and Kubernetes as a container orchestrator to manage the deployment and scaling of the Docker containers across a cluster of servers in a cloud environment. This allows for efficient, reliable, and scalable deployment of applications.

Containerization Enablers ABB is a crucial component in modern software development and deployment processes. It includes the '[Container]' and '[Containers Orchestrator]' elements. The '[Container]' is a lightweight and portable software package that includes everything needed to run an application, ensuring a consistent and isolated environment for applications to run across different computing environments. The '[Containers Orchestrator]' is a software tool or platform that automates the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications, providing a centralized control plane to efficiently manage and coordinate the deployment and operation of containers across a cluster of machines or nodes.
